title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot informs Mr. Barnes that Frank Carter saw the murderer at 12:26, contradicting Carter's earlier claim that he had left the house before Henry Morley was killed. Following several days of investigation, Poirot meets with Alistair Brent at a Gothic mansion to discuss his findings regarding Miss Sainsbury West.

Poirot explains that the identity of the deceased woman is a complex matter. He recounts an encounter on Charlotte Street where he assisted Miss Sainsbury West after her new patent leather shoe, which featured a large decorative buckle, became caught in a taxi door. Later that evening, Miss Sainsbury West disappeared. When Inspector Japp subsequently called Poirot to Leopold King's apartment, they discovered a body inside a fur box. Poirot noted a significant discrepancy: the body wore a very old, worn-out shoe with a hand-sewn buckle, whereas Miss Sainsbury West had been wearing new shoes that same morning.

Poirot details his deduction regarding the victim's identity. He initially considered if the body was Mrs. Albert Chapman, noting that the face had been deliberately destroyed. However, he observed that while Mrs. Chapman wore size 5 shoes, the deceased's shoes fit tightly, suggesting the body was actually Miss Sainsbury West. Despite this, a doctor had testified in court that the body was Mrs. Albert Chapman. Poirot concludes that the woman known as Miss Sainsbury West does not match the honest, naive persona described by her friends in India; instead, her recent behaviors—including lunching with a spy and visiting a woman shortly before a murder—suggest she may be a cold-blooded killer or an accomplice.
